untranslatable_reason: Signal requires calculation of correlation coefficient between primary and companion series, pair-specific threshold generation based on 40th percentile of historical divergence magnitude with correlation adjustment factor, and a custom 'divergence indicator' value. These are composite derived indicators requiring multi-asset correlation analysis and dynamic percentile calculations that cannot be expressed using only the allowed indicators (which operate on single-asset OHLCV data). The signal requires a bespoke indicator unavailable in the standard toolkit.
translation_notes: ARONSON_DRY_0_2 describes a regime-detection signal for normalizing divergence thresholds across asset pairs. The core requirement—correlation-adjusted pair-specific thresholds—cannot be implemented without: (1) multi-asset correlation computation, (2) rolling percentile calculations of divergence magnitude, (3) a 'divergence indicator' not in the allowed set. A simplified version using mean-reversion around SMA_50 is shown above, but this does NOT capture the intended double-channel normalization logic. Requires custom indicator development.
